from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has ICAO code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda o: f"has a leader from the {o} party",
        "capital": lambda o: f"has {o} as its capital",
        "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o.replace(' language','')}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway number {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"has {o} as its largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayNumber": lambda o: f"has a first runway number {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"is in the administrative arrondissement {o}",
        "mayor": lambda o: f"has mayor {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has runway surface {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"official language is {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"people are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses currency {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has headquarters at {o}",
        "class": lambda o: f"belongs to class {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f"belongs to division {o}",
        "order": lambda o: f"belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has the leader title of {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda o: f"has hub airport at {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": lambda o: f"was involved in the battle of {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "countySeat": lambda o: f"has county seat {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"has chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda o: f"has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda o: f"is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject and predicate, combining multiple objects for the same predicate
    from collections import defaultdict
    # subj -> predicate -> list of objects
    subj_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for t in triples:
        subj_pred_objs[t.subject][t.predicate].append(t.object)

    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subj_pred_objs.items():
        phrases: list[str] = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            generator = phrase_map.get(pred)
            if generator:
                if len(objs) == 1:
                    phrase = generator(objs[0])
                else:
                    combined_obj = " and ".join(objs)
                    phrase = generator(combined_obj)
            elif pred.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
                rn = pred.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"has a {rn} runway length of {' and '.join(objs)} feet"
            elif pred.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
                rn = pred.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"has a {rn} runway length of {' and '.join(objs)} metres"
            elif pred.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                rn = pred.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"The {rn} runway has a surface of {' and '.join(objs)}"
            elif pred.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
                rn = pred.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"The {rn} runway is numbered {' and '.join(objs)}"
            else:
                phrase = f"{pred} {' and '.join(objs)}"
            phrases.append(phrase)
        subject_phrases[subj] = phrases

    # Build a single complex sentence from all triples
    all_phrases: list[str] = []
    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        for phrase in phrases:
            all_phrases.append(f"{subj} {phrase}")

    # Join all parts into a single fluent sentence with better flow
    if len(all_phrases) == 1:
        sentence = all_phrases[0] + "."
    elif len(all_phrases) == 2:
        sentence = all_phrases[0] + ", and " + all_phrases[1] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(all_phrases) + "."

    # Ensure the first character is capitalised
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a readable sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    parts: list[str] = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object

        # Specific phrasing for common airport predicates
        if p == "cityServed":
            parts.append(f"{s} serves the city of {o}")

        elif p == "runwayName":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s runway is named {o}")
        elif p == "country":

        elif p == "isPartOf":
            parts.append(f"{s} is part of {o}")
        elif p == "icaoLocationIdentifier":

        elif p == "locationIdentifier":

        elif p == "jurisdiction":
            parts.append(f"{s} has jurisdiction over {o}")
        elif p == "demonym":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s demonym is {o}")
        elif p == "currency":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s currency is {o}")
        elif p == "city":
            parts.append(f"{s} is located in {o}")
        elif p == "headquarter":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s headquarter is {o}")
        elif p == "regionServed":
            parts.append(f"{s} serves the region of {o}")

        elif p == "foundedBy":
            parts.append(f"{s} was founded by {o}")
        elif p == "foundingYear":

        elif p == "postalCode":

            parts.append(f"{s} uses helicopter {o}")
        elif p == "transportAircraft":
            parts.append(f"{s} uses transport aircraft {o}")
        elif p == "aircraftFighter":
            parts.append(f"{s} uses fighter aircraft {o}")
        elif p == "attackAircraft":
            parts.append(f"{s} uses attack aircraft {o}")
        elif p == "battle":
            parts.append(f"{s} was involved in the battle of {o}")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            parts.append(f"{s} has a {rn} runway length of {o} feet")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            parts.append(f"{s} has a {rn} runway length of {o} metres")
        elif p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            parts.append(f"The {rn} runway of {s} has a surface of {o}")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            parts.append(f"The {rn} runway of {s} is numbered {o}")
        else:
            # Generic fallback
            parts.append(f"{s} {p} {o}")

    # Join all parts into a single fluent sentence with better flow
    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0] + "."
    elif len(parts) == 2:
        sentence = parts[0] + ", and " + parts[1] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(parts) + "."
    # Ensure the first character is capitalised
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

The text properly indicates that Greenville is part of both Menasha (town), Wisconsin, and Clayton, Winnebago County, Wisconsin. The text could have been written with fewer semicolons or even combined some of the clauses for a more complex sentence structure, adhering more closely to the preference for one complex sentence, but it does not add extraneous information not present in the triples. Overall, the text meets the accuracy requirement by not adding information that wasn't there and attempts to construct a complex sentence structure.

Based on the review try to understand why the program might have performed poorly and improve the program accordingly.
